item.title: Problem with Footnotes
item.content:encoded: So correct me if I’m wrong but footnote 3 states that death from birth has only occurred once and cites incident-04 but in incident-09 a foundation security person was killed due to birth too… Can we update that please? If so many people have read this as a draft and after posting and that wasn’t found then people might need to read multiple times to find those things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this based on the Apache myth about the vagina house?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hey i actually like this, 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Necroposting is fine if the person posting a comment is furthering the discussion in a meaningful manner, and not just posting something like "lol" or "I agree".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My guess is that OP should have listened to the critiques he received because they made some very good points. The idea deserves an upvote, but it could do so much more with what it has.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not to be a jerk, but try checking the date of a comment before replying to it. Necroposting can get you in some mild troubles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The core concept is good: an old, ugly creep that don't like people in his property. Add a touch of Farm Harem, but tones down the actual danger level to have a skip relatively easy to interact to. I like this idea and the several ways of using it.

But the article actually does nothing with it. Unanswered questions are interesting when they point out to something, but here they point out just to laziness on the Foundation part. How he got the women? How he drugs them? How the anomalous effect occurs? No one looked at the spot where the old jerk got out of the earth?

Is there any test or research at all with this thing?

As far as I can tell, what happened was: Foundation finds house full of kidnapped women and an irate but ultimately harmless old nutjob. For reasons, the Foundation decides to observe the house for 10 months, without any signal of an anomaly nor apparent interest on rescuing people, either, but they also don't call the police. After 10 months, the Foundation decides to rescue his victims and fence his house just 'cause. By accident, the Foundation finds out that he IS, indeed, an anomaly. The Foundation also decides to catalogue the women as part of the anomaly without any research, but still simply release them for good without, again, any research. An Agent provides further insight on what is happening by dying horribly, but still it changes almost nothing whatsoever. Now the Foundation keeps this guy inside a fence without ever doing shit to understand him, except send three teams to try to talk with him. This guy can't do shit to keep the Foundation from doing anything, except attempt suicide, something that can be prevented, but they are good with just letting the jerk be there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's because even it being the same phenomena, the article doesn't consider an agent as a -B.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually the asshole isn't even homicidal. Just an asshole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Small thing - you mention that dying while giving birth isn't usually fatal and only one recorded instance resulted in death, but then the log has two instances of someone dying from the process.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really didn't do much for me. Echoing what TL333s said, this came across as a weaker version of 231 to me, and while there may be some squick factor in giving birth to a tumorous mass that immediately becomes a lipless jerk, it just doesn't really have the impending doom that 231 carried with it to sell the article. And that's a shame, really, as the interview was neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simply Restraining it for good should solve the problem (?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Going to stay neutral on this for now. I like the "old man who just wants his land back" direction you were taking it in, but I feel you aren't going far enough with the idea. The whole mind controlling women for their wombs thing still seems a little unnecessary.
Also you forgot the period here:

Roberts: Hello 2363-A

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the interview, but I can't help thinking this is a watered-down 231. Instead of giving birth to the Scarlet King, they just give birth to a homicidal asshole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's some obvious tonal problems, primarily with medical terminology:

with an apparent growth defect resulting in the absence of lips

Why isn't this just "is afflicted with acheilia"?

Removed instances have been found to be unable to conceive, despite the absence of any apparent problems with their reproductive systems.

How was this found? Why the euphemism?

Overall this is certainly creepy but it is very underdeveloped. How did the women end up there? What is this guy? Why is he acheilious?

ETA: And yet another interview where the moron that the Foundation apparently has assigned to interview this thing insists on giving it classified information for no discernible purpose. This one is especially egregious because it apparently csn escape just by dying.

ETA2; I think on the balance of the above, I will downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: His speech sounds almost exactly like Tom Bombadil's description of himself, adapted for the Foundationverse.

item.title: Re: Yes, but...
item.content:encoded: I tend to approach these things from an engineering viewpoint: Structures creak sometimes. That does not mean that a structure is going to fall down. But one should probably still note the fact that its creaking, because it could mean that its going to fall down.

item.title: Re: Yes, but...
item.content:encoded:
If you don't note that the anomaly turns blue at 3:45 AM on Tuesdays, someone is going to die

In that scenario, it was because the anomaly always turned blue at 3:45 AM on Tuesdays. And even if it wasn't, turning blue usually isn't normal and would be noteworthy in itself.

A lot of things sneeze, so it's not noteworthy unless it's occuring regularly, or it coincides with, say, a rainbow explosion.

Finally, I don't think that's ever been a rule of thumb, just a thing from a tale that can be used to highlight the importance in not leaving out critical information in the procedures about a SCP's properties that can affect how it's contained.

item.title: Yes, but...
item.content:encoded:
If I am an anomaly and I sneeze, the sneeze is not necessarily notable.

Or it could be the only warning that you're about to go supercritical. I forget who said it, but there's an SCP rule of thumb to the effect of "If you don't note that the anomaly turns blue at 3:45 AM on Tuesdays, someone is going to die."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hope poor Goldberry didn't have to put up with this sort of thing.

(I like Tom Bombadil. I don't care that he doesn't make any sense. I like that he doesn't make any sense. That's what I like about this skip too — that he's just there, and he has nothing to do with anything. He's much creepier, but there's a lot less singing.)

item.title: Re: Agreeing with Master Skywalker
item.content:encoded:
Except for the part where it happens in 30 seconds

Just say "Happens in 30 seconds… is otherwise normal."

all parties involved are anomalous

If I am an anomaly and I sneeze, the sneeze is not necessarily notable.

there's a considerable amount of otherwise abnormal stuff going on.

Redundant. See above.

Also, like, I get the feeling you're taking this personally. I'm just making an argument for improving a work of fiction; I really don't mean this to be some kind of attack.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe in every universe, there is a version of Tom Bombadil. Admittedly, this is a part I like about this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get that the idea is not for everyone.

But God damn it I have never regretted a naming choice more than this one. This is not the first comparison to the worst character Tolkien ever wrote, and if I wasn't already stupidly attached to the character I would change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… is this a boogey-man/evil version of Tom Bombadil? Not really doing it for me.

item.title: Re: Agreeing with Master Skywalker
item.content:encoded:
Like I said in the first post, roughly 25 people have reviewed this draft since it's first version, and many of them (and myself) would disagree with the subjective opinion of a few users about the concept of my draft. I can assure you that I am not ignoring your crit or anyone else's, just writing an article that isn't to your taste.

it literally describes typical birth

Except for the part where it happens in 30 seconds, all parties involved are anomalous, and there's a considerable amount of otherwise abnormal stuff going on. The point is that this is quite far from a typical birth, so noting similarities is not uncalled for.

item.title: Re: Agreeing with Master Skywalker
item.content:encoded:
I and many of the other people who read the draft liked the way it was

At least two users, one of them much more experienced than either of us, disagreed. Even if you got nine other users, that's still 20% saying there's stuff wrong with it. I may not be the best judge, but pretty sure Skywalker is someone you should listen to.

Also, "It's stupid" is not particularly helpful or constructive.

That'd be true if the very next words weren't elaborating on "stupid."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I read an earlier draft of this, I get the feeling that you are going for the "farmer who does not like all those interlopers passing by" throughout eternity. I would suggest to work towards that end.

You may want to reconsider the "grow from a random woman" aspect, since how would the SCP recreate itself before the rise of humanity?1 I feel that the body horror aspect drawn from that aspect distracts one from the character that is this SCP.

Footnotes
1. I know you explained it to me once, but I am not convinced that it has to change its methods for every new dominant species. If that were expressed, I will buy it.

item.title: Re: Agreeing with Master Skywalker
item.content:encoded:
I just want to address the fact that this is somewhat unchanged from a version you read: That's true, not entirely, but partially, and that's because I and many of the other people who read the draft liked the way it was, and I personally thought this version would be best and this is the story I wanted to tell.

EDIT: Also, "It's stupid" is not particularly helpful or constructive.

item.title: Agreeing with Master Skywalker
item.content:encoded:
I didn't want to say anything while it was so new, but now that others have had a chance to weigh in…

You seriously didn't change anything I, and others, told you.

  • The birth description is still stupid; it literally describes typical birth-it hurts and you might die.
  • He's still condescending and vague-two creepypasta flags I particularly loathe. Seriously, he's just another spoopy humanoid, with predictably vague, grumpy dialogue.
  • It's dumb that he was able to get that close to the researcher when the Foundation knows he's dangerous and violent.
  • His description is also annoying, cliche, and meh.

This whole thing is "Immortal weirdo what kills you and is ugly"

I no-voted when I thought I was just biased, but now that I've read it a couple more times…
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -1 because it is still what I said in chat: a boogeyman, not much else.

item.title: Re: Interesting story
item.content:encoded: I mean that's the gist of it. What do you want to know?

item.title: Interesting story
item.content:encoded: Could you describe SCP-2363-A more? Or is he just a creepy looking old man with exposed teeth and cataracts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's an article I've been shopping around for a while.

Special thanks to the roughly two dozen people who took a look at this over the last month, I'd list you all but I'm sure I'd forget someone.
